policy_name: Export control on Lithium ore in Zimbabwe
description: IEA policy page states Zimbabwe prohibited exports of lithium bearing ore or unbeneficiated lithium in December 2022 through the Base Minerals Export Control Order; exports require written permit from the Minister.

IEA policy page states that on 10 June 2025 Zimbabwe announced a ban on lithium concentrates as of 1 January 2027; from that date only lithium sulphates and higher products will be allowed to be exported.
IEA policy page describes Zimbabwe export permit and beneficiation requirements for lithium exports, including conditions for beneficiated lithium and export permits.
IEA policy page states Zimbabwe designates strategic minerals including lithium, copper, rare earth minerals and nickel under the Mines and Minerals Act framework.

policy_name: Amendments to Zimbabwe's Mining Tax Regime
description: IEA policy page describes changes including a 2 percent levy on gross value of domestic and export sales of lithium and other minerals, plus mining royalty provisions.

IEA policy page states Chile's National Lithium Strategy increases state participation in lithium development and sets the framework for exploiting lithium through the State, state-owned companies, administrative concessions or special operation contracts.
IEA policy page states Canada released its Critical Minerals Strategy to increase responsibly sourced critical minerals supply and support global value chains.
